title: molten Polish Fish Pilaf
description: A rich, briny, nostalgic Polish dish featuring fish and a crispy tadka. This recipe is low-sodium-friendly and perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the arborio rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the low-sodium fish stock, 1 cup at a time, stirring continuously and allowing each cup to be fully absorbed before adding the next.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been fully absorbed.
While the rice is cooking, prepare the tadka by heating the remaining 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the caraway seeds and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fish fillets to the tadka and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Flake the cooked fish into large pieces and stir into the cooked rice.
Stir in the dried parsley and salt-free seasoning blend.
Serve the molten Polish Fish Pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
